from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects about universities (or related entities)
    into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build a mapping from subject to its triples
    subject_map = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_map:
            subject_map[t.subject] = []
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Identify the main subject (typically a university/institution)
    main_subject = None
    for subj in subject_map:
        if "University" in subj or "College" in subj or "Institute" in subj or "School" in subj:
            main_subject = subj
            break

    if main_subject is None and subject_map:
        main_subject = list(subject_map.keys())[0]

    main_triples = subject_map.get(main_subject, [])

    # Collect all related entities and their triples
    related_entities = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.object not in related_entities and t.object != main_subject:
            related_entities[t.object] = []
        if t.object != main_subject:
            related_entities[t.object].append(t)

    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase template.
    templates = {
        "city": "is located in {object}",
        "country": "is in {object}",
        "latinName": "has the Latin name \"{object}\"",
        "nickname": "is nicknamed {object}",
        "rector": "has {object} as rector",
        "academicStaffSize": "has an academic staff of {object}",
        "established": "was established in {object}",
        "state": "is in the state of {object}",
        "governmentType": "has a government type of {object}",
        "hasToItsNortheast": "has {object} to its northeast",
        "dean": "has {object} as dean",
        "location": "is located in {object}",
        "numberOfStudents": "has {object} students",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with {object}",
        "director": "has {object} as director",
        "motto": "has the motto \"{object}\"",
        "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": "has {object} postgraduate students",
        "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": "has {object} undergraduate students",
        "officialSchoolColour": "has official school colours {object}",
        "outlookRanking": "is ranked {object} in the Outlook ranking",
        "sportsOffered": "offers {object}",
        "wasGivenTheTechnicalCampusStatusBy": "was given technical campus status by {object}",
        "founder": "was founded by {object}",
        "leader": "is led by {object}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {object}",
        "religion": "follows {object}",
        "headquarter": "has headquarter in {object}",
        "largestCity": "has {object} as largest city",
        "river": "has the river {object}",
        "hasToItsWest": "has {object} to its west",
        "anthem": "has anthem \"{object}\"",
        "ethnicGroup": "includes {object} as ethnic group",
        "legislature": "has legislature {object}",
        "sportGoverningBody": "has sport governing body {object}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {object}",
        "neighboringMunicipality": "neighbors {object}",
        "capital": "{object} is the capital",
        "patronSaint": "has {object} as patron saint",
        "hasToItsNorthwest": "has {object} to its northwest",
        "campus": "has campus at {object}",
        "president": "has {object} as president",
        "staff": "has {object} staff members",
        "numberOfDoctoralStudents": "has {object} doctoral students",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is at an elevation of {object} meters above sea level",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {object}",
        "longName": "has long name \"{object}\"",
    }

    # Build the sentence starting with main subject
    clauses = []

    # First clause: main subject with primary attributes
    primary_attrs = []
    secondary_attrs = []

    for t in main_triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, "has {predicate} {object}")
        clause = tmpl.format(object=t.object)
        if t.predicate in ["city", "country", "state", "established"]:
            primary_attrs.append(clause)
        else:
            secondary_attrs.append(clause)

    # Build the main sentence
    sentence_parts = [main_subject]

    # Add primary attributes in a connected way
    if primary_attrs:
        if len(primary_attrs) == 1:
            sentence_parts.append(primary_attrs[0])
        else:
            # Connect multiple primary attributes
            sentence_parts.append(primary_attrs[0])
            for attr in primary_attrs[1:]:
                sentence_parts.append(f", {attr}")

    # Add secondary attributes with "and" or commas
    if secondary_attrs:
        if len(secondary_attrs) == 1:
            sentence_parts.append(f" and {secondary_attrs[0]}")
        else:
            sentence_parts.append(f", {secondary_attrs[0]}")
            for attr in secondary_attrs[1:]:
                sentence_parts.append(f", {attr}")

    # Join all parts into a single complex sentence
    sentence = "".join(sentence_parts)
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."

    # Ensure the sentence starts with a capital letter
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] if sentence else ""

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
